excerpts:
Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

excerpts: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents. This integration enables all customers to use their existing phone systems while leveraging ElevenLabs’ advanced voice AI capabilities.
SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents.
Leverage encrypted TLS transport and media encryption for enhanced security

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier

Key points from the sources show that ElevenLabs offers: a complete stack for voice agents including text-to-speech with many voices and languages, speech-to-text, and a suite of APIs/SDKs for TTS/STT and tools integration; low-latency streaming capabilities (partial transcripts and TTS streaming) and a WebSocket/Webhook ecosystem for real-time events; a dedicated Tools system to call external APIs; and support for dynamic variables to personalize prompts and greetings. Security posture features (private deployments, HIPAA considerations) and compliance coverage are mentioned, alongside post-call analytics and webhook-driven integrations. The Retell-specific capability of weaving tool_call_invocation/results into transcripts and a dedicated metadata event for frontend navigation is not described as a native feature in ElevenLabs; instead, those behaviors would require custom stitching or using ElevenLabs’ Events/Webhooks in conjunction with the frontend channel. Additionally, Retell’s WebSocket metadata event for server-to-frontend data transfer has no one-to-one equivalent in ElevenLabs, suggesting reimplementation if exact parity is desired. The portability assessment thus supports most generalized capabilities (streams, tool calls, dynamic variables, webhooks) but notes the absence of exact native equivalents for Retell’s precise transcript weaving and metadata passthrough, implying significant architectural work for a true feature-for-feature port.

excerpts:
Dynamic variables | ElevenLabs Documentation
Dynamic variables allow you to inject runtime values into your agent's messages, system prompts, and tools. This enables you to personalize each
Dynamic variables can be integrated into multiple aspects of your agent:
**System prompts** to customize behavior and context
**First messages** to personalize greetings
**Tool parameters and headers** to pass user-specific data
**Personalizing greetings** with user names
**Including account details** in responses
**Passing data** to tool calls
**Customizing behavior** based on subscription tiers
**Accessing system information** like conversation ID or call duration

Dynamic variables are ideal for injecting user-specific data that shouldn't be hardcoded into your
agent's configuration.

This provides the granular, synchronous/near-synchronous eventing capability that your fine-grained field highlights as distinct from standard webhooks. Specifically, Retell’s WebSocket description makes clear it “controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call” and that server-side events include references to tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, and transcript weaving. This establishes the high-granularity, event-driven flow that is central to the finegrained value. On the ElevenLabs side, the material confirms the existence of a general Events system that subscribers can use to receive conversation updates in real time, and that these events can trigger Webhooks for external processing, which demonstrates a robust asynchronous eventing pathway. The ElevenLabs materials also describe the WebSocket-capable tools and streaming capabilities (WebSocket-based streaming for transcripts and TTS, and a Tools API for calling external actions), which supports a broader but less granular stream of events compared to Retell’s in-call WebSocket. Webhook overviews for Retell and ElevenLabs corroborate that both ecosystems expose webhook mechanisms to deliver events to external systems, though Retell emphasizes in-call, transcript-level granularity via the LLM WebSocket, while ElevenLabs emphasizes real-time conversation events and the ability to push those events to webhooks for downstream processing. The combination of these excerpts supports a comparison where Retell provides finer-grained, per-utterance and tool-call-aware transcripts and events via a persistent WebSocket, whereas ElevenLabs provides broader, real-time conversation events and webhook-enabled integrations that cover general state changes rather than per-utterance tool-call detail. The additional references to post-call analysis, webhook overview, and platform-specific webhook capabilities further contextualize how each ecosystem exposes their eventing to external consumers, reinforcing the overall assessment of granular versus broader eventing capabilities. In summary, the most compelling support highlights Retell’s transcript-with-tool-call weaving and the rich in-call event set via WebSocket, while ElevenLabs provides a strong real-time Events system with webhook triggers for asynchronous processing, complemented by its own streaming and WebSocket features for real-time audio/text workflows.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

nd_call
boolean
If set to true, Retell would end the call after content associated with this id is fully spoken. If agent was interrupted during speaking, the end call signal would get discarded.
​
transfer_number
string
If set, Retell would transfer the call to the number specified here after content associated with this id is fully spoken. Only applicable to Retell numbers or imported numbers. If your voice agent is using custom telephony via the dial to SIP endpoint route, you need to write your own call transfer logic.
​
digit_to_press
string
If set, Retell would press the input digit or digits to send DTMF tones after content associated with this id is fully spoken (although you probably do not want the voice agent to speak anything when pressing digits). Only applicable if you are already using latest Call API V2

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

excerpts:
LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

basis.reasoning: Retell AI exposes a tightly coupled tool-calling observable model through its LLM WebSocket protocol. When the LLM logic decides to call a tool, a dedicated tool_call_invocation event is sent to Retell, and after the tool completes, a tool_call_result event is sent back. Retell can weave these invocation and result events into the final transcript, creating a precise, timed audit trail that interleaves tool activity with dialogue. This live, on-wire reporting enables low-latency observability and auditability of tool usage during a conversation. In addition, Retell documents a WebSocket flow where the server can send various events (config, ping_pong, response, agent_interrupt, tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, metadata) to coordinate and annotate the call, and to push transcripts that include tool-call data when configured. This combination supports explicit, real-time bookkeeping of tool usage and transcript augmentation. Separately, Retell describes a Transcript of the call woven with tool call invocations and results, including a transcription that marks when tools were invoked and what the results were, which is exactly the kind of integrated trace the field value describes. On the ElevenLabs side, the platform exposes a Tools feature that enables agents to call external APIs or clients, with tool definitions configurable within the Agents platform and parameter/header passing via dynamic variables. This is a higher-level abstraction, managed by the Agents platform rather than low-level WebSocket events, and coverage of real-time tool invocation would rely on the platform’s Events and Webhooks rather than the same interleaved transcript model. ElevenLabs also provides WebSocket and webhook capabilities for streaming and event-driven workflows (e.g., WebSockets for streaming, and Tools and Events to trigger actions and report outcomes). In addition, ElevenLabs documents a set of core capabilities for building integrations (WebSocket API for custom implementations, and a Connect tools capability to enable agents to call clients & APIs). These pieces collectively imply that Retell emphasizes tightly integrated, timestamped tool invocation events and transcript weaving, while ElevenLabs emphasizes modular, platform-managed tool calls with event-driven reporting, which may require stitching transcripts and events post-hoc to achieve a similar level of synchronized visibility. Finally, both ecosystems offer webhook-style mechanisms to surface events and some form of transcript-related data, with Retell providing explicit transcript weaving and real-time tool event events, and ElevenLabs offering Webhooks and tool-related events to expose actions and outcomes.

basis.reasoning: The Retell WebSocket protocol exposes dedicated events for controlling interruptions, including an agent_interrupt event that can be sent at any time to halt the agent’s current speech, enabling immediate reactions to user barge-in. It also defines tool_call_invocation and tool_call_result events for weaving tool usage into transcripts, and a metadata event to pass contextual frontend data, all of which contribute to fine-grained control over when and how the agent speaks or interrupts. Additionally, the protocol uses turn-taking markers (agent_turn and user_turn) to time backend actions, such as initiating tool calls, within a given conversational turn. This combination provides low-level, imperative control over interruptions and turn-taking during a live conversation. In contrast, ElevenLabs approaches interruptions and turn-taking in a more declarative, configuration-driven way through the conversation flow settings, which specify how interruptions are handled and what timeouts apply, abstracting away real-time control from the developer. Regarding latency, Retell emphasizes core voice infrastructure and the impact of the developer’s LLM and tool calls on end-to-end latency, while ElevenLabs emphasizes ultra-low latency in core components: partial transcripts can appear in roughly 150 ms, and the TTS streaming pathway is designed to begin streaming audio with minimal delay, significantly affecting Time to First Byte (TTFB). The combination of Retell’s fine-grained, event-driven interruption control and ElevenLabs’ streaming-focused latency optimizations highlights a fundamental difference in how each platform enables real-time interruption handling and latency management in conversational AI agents. In summary, Retell provides explicit agent_interrupt events and turn-taking markers for precise interruption control, plus a do_not_interrupt flag to protect certain utterances from barge-in, while ElevenLabs offers lower-level latency-focused streaming capabilities and a declarative interruption model via conversation flow configuration. The cited excerpts collectively support that Retell delivers high-granularity interruption control and active latency management through its WebSocket protocol, whereas ElevenLabs relies more on streaming latency optimizations and high-level flow definitions to manage interruptions and timing.

excerpts: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents. This integration enables all customers to use their existing phone systems while leveraging ElevenLabs’ advanced voice AI capabilities.
SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents.
Leverage encrypted TLS transport and media encryption for enhanced security

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier
citations.title: SIP trunking | ElevenLabs Documentation

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.
citations.title: LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs

basis.reasoning: The Retell WebSocket documentation describes a dedicated LLM WebSocket that connects your server at call start to stream transcripts, handle tool call invocations, and exchange control messages such as agent interrupts and transcript events. This is a core part of Retell’s API surface and is explicitly designed to carry tool invocation and results, transcripts woven with tool calls, and various event types including config, ping_pong, response, agent_interrupt, tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, and metadata. These details establish Retell's distinctive, stateful WebSocket-based integration model and its emphasis on streaming transcripts, call control, and tool integration. Related excerpts further describe how Retell supports a custom function pathway (function calling) and the ability to integrate with external systems via custom functions, reinforcing the notion of action-oriented capabilities that can be invoked from the LLM. This is complemented by the Retell SDKs and REST API surfaces which offer official Node.js and Python SDKs for server-side integration, error handling, environment configuration, and type safety, illustrating a complete developer experience around building and deploying Retell-powered agents. In contrast, ElevenLabs documents a broader, multi-layered approach: an Agents platform that coordinates core components (ASR, LLM, TTS, and a turn-taking model) and provides APIs/SDKs for text-to-speech, speech-to-text, voice cloning, and other audio-related capabilities; a React SDK and a Swift SDK for frontend/mobile integration; and a WebSocket API for low-level streaming of STT/TTS as well as a separate Tools/Knowledge Base/Conversation Flow documentation that supports custom integrations like WebSocket-based streams and webhooks. The ElevenLabs materials explicitly discuss WebSocket usage for streaming and real-time events, a dedicated WebSocket API for streaming transcripts, and notes on low-latency, streaming partial transcripts, as well as an ecosystem of APIs for models, tools, and webhooks. When evaluating whether a Retell-like Portfolios integration could be supported by ElevenLabs, the key observations are that ElevenLabs exposes high-level, end-to-end agent orchestration capabilities plus lower-level WebSocket streams for STT/TTS and a path for custom integrations via WebSockets, REST endpoints, events, and webhooks. The ElevenLabs content also highlights SDKs centered on frontend integration and mentions tools for calling APIs and external systems, which aligns with the need to support tool calls and metadata, albeit within a different architectural model than Retell’s dedicated LLM WebSocket protocol. Therefore, the strongest direct support for the field value comes from explicit Retell WebSocket documentation and function-calling integration details, followed by concrete mentions of SDKs and WebSocket streaming capabilities from both platforms, and finally ElevenLabs’ explicit references to tools, models, and webhooks that enable integration. The overall assessment will weigh Retell’s dedicated streaming WebSocket design as the most direct match to the field value, with supporting context from related documentation about SDKs and function calling, while recognizing ElevenLabs’ parallel but differently structured capabilities that cover similar integration patterns through WebSockets, REST, and events.

This is evidenced by explicit mentions of the LLM WebSocket connecting to your server, Retell initiating the WebSocket at call start, and the server handling events such as tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, metadata, and the ability to weave transcripts with tool invocations. The documentation also describes a transcript that is woven with tool call invocations and results, and outlines the kinds of events that can be sent (config, ping_pong, response, agent_interrupt, tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, metadata). These pieces together support the claim that Retell provides a developer-centric, server-controlled LLM interaction model with rich event semantics and auditability, suitable for integrating telephony and web calling while keeping full control of LLM logic on the server side. Supporting evidence also shows that there is a general function-calling capability that allows the agent to invoke external systems via custom functions, further enabling back-end control and integration points. On the ElevenLabs side, the platform is described as an end-to-end conversational AI platform with built-in, high-quality STT (Scribe v2 Realtime) and extensive TTS/voice cloning, plus a turnkey toolset for knowledge bases and a Tools system for function calling, plus broad integration surfaces like native SIP trunking, Twilio integration, React and Swift SDKs, and embeddable web widgets. There are explicit references to streaming support for transcripts in real time, low-latency streaming, and dynamic variables that can be injected at runtime, which align with an emphasis on rapid deployment and rich audio/voice capabilities. Additional supporting excerpts describe a knowledge base for RAG, and a Tools subsystem that enables agents to call clients and APIs to perform actions, which together illustrate ElevenLabs’ all-in-one platform approach with higher-level abstractions. In summary, Retell’s strength is developer-centric control with a configurable WebSocket protocol and explicit event semantics for turn-taking, tool calls, and metadata, while ElevenLabs emphasizes an integrated platform with strong voice quality, streaming, and built-in tooling for RAG and actions, plus broad ecosystem bindings. The confidence reflects that the core contrasts are well-supported by direct descriptions across Retell’s LLM WebSocket documentation and the ElevenLabs agent/docs sections on streaming, tools, and integration surfaces.

excerpts:
LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

excerpts: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents. This integration enables all customers to use their existing phone systems while leveraging ElevenLabs’ advanced voice AI capabilities.
SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents.
Leverage encrypted TLS transport and media encryption for enhanced security

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier

basis.reasoning: The migration risk assessment hinges on several concrete technical gaps highlighted across the sources. First, Retell’s LLM WebSocket model bundles transcripts, turn-taking signals, and multiple control events in a single, server-initiated channel. Re-architecting this for ElevenLabs would require splitting responsibilities across Agent tools, Events, and Workflows, and potentially introducing a separate client-initiated WebSocket layer for streaming STT and TTS. This supports the claim that a WebSocket protocol mismatch will be a core challenge, because the architecture differences demand rethinking the end-to-end communication and control flow between frontend and backend. Second, Retell weaves tool_call_invocation and tool_call_result events into the transcript with precise timing; replicating that level of auditability would necessitate a new event-driven pipeline (capturing tool execution via Events/Webhooks and a post-processing service to merge with transcripts) to meet similar fidelity on ElevenLabs. Third, the front-end metadata channel that Retell transmits via a dedicated metadata event would need a new solution in ElevenLabs, such as an alternate eventing system, frontend subscriptions via a backend proxy, or leveraging ElevenLabs’ Events/Webhooks to convey similar metadata. Fourth, telephony integration requires validating codec compatibility (G.711/G.722), audio resampling needs, and TLS/SRTP security configurations when bridging to SIP trunks, which implies re-validating security and media pathways for ElevenLabs. Fifth, there are related capabilities around turn-taking and interruptions, streaming transcripts, and real-time audio streaming via WebSockets in both Retell and ElevenLabs contexts, which influence latency, synchronization, and control semantics during migration. Finally, the existence of function calling concepts and their integration points in both ecosystems suggests a need to align or replace Retell’s function-calling behavior with ElevenLabs’ tools or events, ensuring consistent behavior for tool invocation, results, and downstream processing. In summary, the excerpts collectively indicate that the migration will face architectural reconciliation of streaming and control channels, auditing of tool invocations, frontend metadata propagation, telephony/media handling, and alignment of function-calling semantics with ElevenLabs constructs, all of which are critical for a successful migration strategy.

basis.reasoning: The fine-grained field value centers on programmatic, real-time call routing and control via an LLM WebSocket API, including specific directives like transferring a call to a number and ending the call. The Retell AI LLM WebSocket documentation explicitly describes a server-driven WebSocket that sends and handles events such as config, ping_pong, response, agent_interrupt, tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, and metadata, which directly demonstrates real-time control of call flow and the ability to initiate, interrupt, or conclude interactions. This aligns with the field’s emphasis on dynamic, context-aware call handling and the ability to issue targeted directives during a conversation. Additional support comes from the Retell docs that describe server-led events to manage how the agent interacts, which would include initiating transfers or terminations as part of the call lifecycle. ElevenLabs’ SIP trunking materials show how telephony infrastructure can be connected to the platform, establishing the transport layer for telephony-based routing, while references to the ElevenLabs “Tools” and “Workflows” indicate programmatic action triggers and dynamic variables that influence routing decisions (e.g., personalized greetings or data passed to external systems). These pieces collectively support the notion that call routing and control can be programmatically steered through APIs and event-driven mechanisms. The ElevenAgents documentation’s emphasis on controlling conversation flow, including turn-taking, interruptions, and timeout settings, further corroborates the principle of programmable routing/flow control within the platform. The SIP headers guidance (for call identification and traceability) provides a practical mechanism to forward and correlate calls in routed scenarios, reinforcing how routing decisions tie into telephony events. Websocket-based streaming references (for client-side and server-side transcripts) illustrate the underlying communication channels that enable real-time control and visibility of call state, which is essential for implementing the described control directives. The remaining excerpts provide contextual support about webhook triggering, knowledge integration for grounding, and general setup steps that, while not central to the precise control directives, sketch the broader ecosystem that makes such programmable routing feasible.

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

excerpts: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents.
Leverage encrypted TLS transport and media encryption for enhanced security

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier

basis.reasoning: Direct descriptions of webhook and eventing systems are used to anchor the comparison at the level of granularity and reliability. The webhook overview explains how events are delivered in real time to external systems, which is essential to understanding the baseline reliability and push-model behavior. The ElevenLabs webhook excerpt confirms the existence of a configurable eventing surface, contributing to a broad, asynchronous update model. In contrast, the Retell AI LLM WebSocket excerpts describe a persistent, bi-directional channel that carries live transcripts, turn-taking markers, and tool-call related events, enabling fine-grained, synchronous control and auditing of interactions. The server-to-LLM interaction schema (config, ping_pong, response, agent_interrupt, tool_call_invocation, tool_call_result, metadata) illustrates how granular and timely the signals can be, which supports a higher level of traceability for in-call actions than typical webhook streams. The transcripts of calls woven with tool-call invocations (and the related config flag transcript_with_tool_calls) further indicate that Retell offers precise timing and content details for tool usage, which is a level of detail not typically provided by standard webhook models. Turn-taking signals (agent_turn or user_turn) show clear synchronization cues that are valuable for auditing and coordinating downstream actions. Additional context about metadata events in the LLM WebSocket and the ability to pass frontend-facing data via metadata reinforces the notion of a tightly-coupled, signal-rich channel for in-call processing. While the ElevenLabs tools and knowledge-base-related references establish a general, event-based ecosystem, they do not demonstrate the same in-call granularity and synchronous auditing capabilities described for Retell. Overall, Retell’s WebSocket approach provides higher granularity and timely, structured events for per-utterance and per-tool interactions, whereas ElevenLabs emphasizes broader, asynchronous eventing suitable for standard webhook-based integrations. The confidence in this assessment is medium because the excerpts clearly describe different architectures and capabilities, but a direct, explicit one-to-one feature-for-feature mapping would require more detailed specifications from both platforms.

excerpts:
LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us

LLM WebSocket
Arguments for this tool call, it's a stringified JSON object. ​. Tool Call Result Event. If you send the tool call invocation and results in the websocket

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

nd_call
boolean
If set to true, Retell would end the call after content associated with this id is fully spoken. If agent was interrupted during speaking, the end call signal would get discarded.
​
transfer_number
string
If set, Retell would transfer the call to the number specified here after content associated with this id is fully spoken. Only applicable to Retell numbers or imported numbers. If your voice agent is using custom telephony via the dial to SIP endpoint route, you need to write your own call transfer logic.
​
digit_to_press
string
If set, Retell would press the input digit or digits to send DTMF tones after content associated with this id is fully spoken (although you probably do not want the voice agent to speak anything when pressing digits). Only applicable if you are already using latest Call API V2

Collectively, these excerpts substantiate the key aspects of ElevenLabs’ STT performance and surrounding capabilities that the field value emphasizes, while also offering necessary context about streaming, latency handling, and integration points.

ElevenAgents coordinates 4 core components:
A fine-tuned Speech to Text (ASR) model for speech recognition
Your choice of language model or [custom](/docs/eleven-agents/customization/llm/custom-llm) LLM
A low-latency Text to Speech (TTS) model across 5k+ voices and 70+ languages

Collectively, these excerpts support the claim that Retell provides webhooks for call lifecycle events, while granular live content (transcripts, tool calls, and related turn-taking) is handled over the LLM WebSocket, forming a clear division between webhook notifications and WebSocket-driven streaming.

excerpts:
Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

nd_call
boolean
If set to true, Retell would end the call after content associated with this id is fully spoken. If agent was interrupted during speaking, the end call signal would get discarded.
​
transfer_number
string
If set, Retell would transfer the call to the number specified here after content associated with this id is fully spoken. Only applicable to Retell numbers or imported numbers. If your voice agent is using custom telephony via the dial to SIP endpoint route, you need to write your own call transfer logic.
​
digit_to_press
string
If set, Retell would press the input digit or digits to send DTMF tones after content associated with this id is fully spoken (although you probably do not want the voice agent to speak anything when pressing digits). Only applicable if you are already using latest Call API V2

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

excerpts: Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

LLM WebSocket
Arguments for this tool call, it's a stringified JSON object. ​. Tool Call Result Event. If you send the tool call invocation and results in the websocket

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

**Tool parameters and headers** to pass user-specific data

excerpts: Leverage encrypted TLS transport and media encryption for enhanced security

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.
citations.title: LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

basis.reasoning: The fine-grained field value seeks confirmation that the ElevenLabs platform supports Custom LLM Integration (Bring Your Own LLM) to power agent logic and responses. The most directly supporting information describes a WebSocket interface that connects your server to the Retell AI system, with Retell initiating the WebSocket and your server handling live transcripts and responses, effectively enabling your own LLM to drive agent behavior. This demonstrates a clear BYOLLM pathway via real-time data exchange and control over agent outputs. Further, the event-focused documentation specifies server-originated events such as tool_call_invocation and tool_call_result, indicating that tool invocations and their outcomes can be woven into transcripts, which is essential for integrating an external LLM that can issue and respond to tool calls. Additional descriptions outline a WebSocket API for custom implementations and a dedicated event spec for transcript and tool-call weaving, reinforcing the feasibility of BYOLLM integration. In more general terms, there are references to a low-level WebSocket protocol for custom implementations and to real-time streaming and metadata events, which collectively support the concept of plugging in an external LLM to power agent logic and manage interactions in real time.

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket.

basis.reasoning: The metadata passthrough capability is directly described as an optional metadata event that passes data from the server to the frontend during a web call, which is then forwarded to the frontend for UI updates or state synchronization. This aligns exactly with the requested field value, which identifies the capability area as Transcript & Data Handling and the feature as Metadata Passthrough, noting that the platform supports a metadata event that carries data from the server via the LLM WebSocket to the frontend. Additionally, the surrounding WebSocket documentation confirms that the LLM WebSocket is the conduit for real-time interactions and that the session is initiated by Retell at call start, establishing the channel through which metadata can be transmitted. Together, these excerpts support the presence of a metadata event on the LLM WebSocket and its forwarding behavior to the frontend, which is the core of the requested field value. The excerpts also provide context that there is a defined event taxonomy (including a metadata event) within the WebSocket communication and how it fits into the overall call lifecycle, reinforcing the interpretation of Metadata Passthrough as a feature in Transcript & Data Handling.

excerpts:
Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

basis.reasoning: The most directly relevant excerpts establish the core streaming capabilities: a WebSocket-based API for client-side streaming that delivers partial transcripts as you speak and committed transcripts when segments are complete, and server-side streaming that processes audio streams with transcript control. These directly support the claim of simultaneous STT streaming and finalization behavior. Additional excerpts confirm the Scribe v2 Realtime STT model with ultra-low latency around 150ms and support for streaming audio in chunks while receiving transcripts in real time, which aligns with the stated latency and streaming capabilities. The TTS side is covered by excerpts describing a WebSocket API designed for minimal latency that begins streaming audio as partial text input is received, and an example showing streaming tokens from an OpenAI model to the TTS WebSocket to initiate playback almost immediately. This collection of excerpts collectively substantiates the main points of the field value: dual streaming capabilities (STT and TTS), low latency streaming, and WebSocket-based streams. excerpts:
turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.
citations.title: LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs

excerpts: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents. This integration enables all customers to use their existing phone systems while leveraging ElevenLabs’ advanced voice AI capabilities.
SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents.

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier
Leverage encrypted TLS transport and media encryption for enhanced security

SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents. This integration enables all customers to use their existing phone systems while leveraging ElevenLabs’ advanced voice AI capabilities.
SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) trunking allows you to connect your existing telephony infrastructure directly to ElevenLabs Agents.

To ensure proper forwarding and traceability of call metadata, include the following custom SIP headers in your webhook payload and SIP INVITE request:
**X-CALL-ID**: Unique identifier for the call
**X-CALLER-ID**: Identifier for the calling party

If the standard headers above are not present, the system will automatically look for the Twilio-specific SIP header:
**sip.twilio.callSid**: Twilio's unique call identifier

excerpts:
Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

excerpts:
turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call.
