Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

turntaking
enum<string>
Indicates change of speaker (turn taking). This field will be present when speaker changes to user (user turn), or right before agent is about to speak (agent turn). This field can be helpful determining when to call functions in the call. Available options: `agent_turn` , `user_turn`
citations.title: LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs

excerpts: Webhooks allow your application to receive real-time notifications about events that occur in your Retell AI account. Instead of continuously polling our API, webhooks push data to your application as events happen, making your integrations more efficient and responsive.

excerpts:
Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

excerpts:
Your Server -> Retell Event Spec
There will be a couple events that your server can send to Retell in this websocket, to differentiate between them, set the `response_type` field:
`config` : (optional) the initial config for configuring reconnection, whether to send call details etc
`ping_pong` : (optional) to check for disconnection and keep the connection alive
`response` : (required) to send back responses to the user when requested
`agent_interrupt` : (optional) to jump in conversation and speak content in it immediately, interrupts both
agent and user.
`tool_call_invocation` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`tool_call_result` : (optional) to bookkeep and weave tool call invocations and results in the transcript.
`metadata` : (optional) to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call.

Transcript of the call weaved with tool call invocation and results. Populated when `transcript_with_tool_calls` field is set to true in the config event . It precisely captures when (at what utterance, which word) the tool was invoked and what was the result if the tool calls were sent timely in this LLM websocket. See tool call invocation event and tool call result event for more information of how to send tool call invocations and results. See `transcript_with_tool_calls` field of Get Call API Response for the detailed schema for the object in the list.

Metadata Event
Sometimes you may wish to pass some data from the server where the LLM is running to the frontend during a web call, for animation purposes or other stuff. It can be challenging to make sure the frontend of the call is connecting from can connect to the server where the LLM is running. In this case, you can send metadata events to the Retell and Retell will forward it to the frontend. See frontend metadata event for the forwarded event.

Overview
This socket shall connect directly to your server, where you would get live transcript and other relevant inputs from us, and provide responses back to us using your custom LLM. In short, this WebSocket controls what the agents says, and controls actions like ending the call. Retell AI will initiate this WebSocket when starting the call, and your server should get prepared to handle it.
citations.title: LLM WebSocket - Retell AI Docs

executive_summary.description: A high-level overview comparing Retell AI and ElevenLabs Agents in 2026, summarizing their key strengths, primary use cases, and major differentiators.

custom_agent_portability_assessment.description: A detailed assessment of whether a custom Retell-integrated portfolio voice agent, with all its specified functions (OpenAI Agents SDK streaming, tool-call events, metadata for navigation, etc.), can be fully supported and replicated on the ElevenLabs Agents platform.

capability_area.description: The domain of the capability, such as Telephony, Custom LLM, or Analytics.

feature.description: The specific feature within the capability area.

description.description: A detailed description of the feature and its function.

retell_ai_capabilities_overview.description: A summary of Retell AI's core platform capabilities, including its architecture, primary features, and technical specifications across various domains like telephony, API, and voice synthesis.

capability_area.description: The domain of the capability, such as Telephony, Conversational AI, or Voice Synthesis.

feature.description: The specific feature within the capability area.

description.description: A detailed description of the feature and its function.

elevenlabs_agents_capabilities_overview.description: A summary of ElevenLabs Agents' core platform capabilities, detailing its architecture, primary features, and technical specifications, particularly focusing on its conversational AI, voice, and integration features.

feature_differences_comparison.description: A general comparison of the overall feature sets of Retell AI and ElevenLabs Agents, highlighting unique or divergent functionalities.

retell_ai_sdks.description: Details on Retell AI's official SDKs (e.g., Node.js, Python) and their capabilities.

elevenlabs_agents_sdks.description: Details on ElevenLabs Agents' SDKs (e.g., React, Swift) and their focus.

api_style.description: Comparison of the primary API interaction styles (e.g., REST vs. WebSocket-centric).

documentation_quality.description: An assessment of the clarity, completeness, and usability of each platform's API documentation.

retell_ai_telephony.description: How Retell AI handles telephony, including custom telephony via SIP and number purchasing.

elevenlabs_agents_telephony.description: How ElevenLabs Agents handle telephony, focusing on SIP trunking and Twilio integration.

web_calling_support.description: Comparison of support for web-based calling, including WebRTC and frontend SDKs.

call_routing_and_control.description: Comparison of features for call routing, transferring, and management.

retell_ai_integration.description: Details of Retell AI's LLM WebSocket protocol for deep, real-time control over the agent's responses and actions.

elevenlabs_agents_integration.description: Details of how ElevenLabs supports custom LLMs and its WebSocket API for streaming STT and TTS.

communication_protocol.description: A direct comparison of the WebSocket event specifications and the level of control offered by each.

retell_ai_implementation.description: How Retell AI handles tool calls via specific 'tool_call_invocation' and 'tool_call_result' WebSocket events.

elevenlabs_agents_implementation.description: How ElevenLabs Agents implement 'Tools' to interact with external systems and APIs.

real_time_invocation.description: Comparison of the ability to trigger and report on tool calls in real-time during the conversation.

retell_ai_streaming.description: Details on Retell's live transcript streaming, including the weaving of tool call events.

elevenlabs_agents_streaming.description: Details on ElevenLabs' client-side and server-side streaming for STT, and its TTS streaming API.

data_format_and_access.description: Comparison of the data formats and schemas used for streamed transcripts and audio.

retell_ai_handling.description: Analysis of Retell AI's barge-in capabilities and 'agent_interrupt' event for fine-grained control.

elevenlabs_agents_handling.description: Analysis of ElevenLabs Agents' turn-taking model and conversation flow settings for managing interruptions.

latency_optimization.description: Comparison of features and techniques each platform uses to minimize conversational latency.

stt_performance.description: Comparison of the Speech-to-Text (STT) models, including accuracy, speed, and latency (e.g., ElevenLabs' Scribe v2).

tts_quality_and_options.description: Comparison of the Text-to-Speech (TTS) quality, naturalness, and variety of available voices.

voice_cloning_capability.description: Assessment of the voice cloning features, including ease of use, quality of the cloned voice, and API access.

retell_ai_implementation.description: Details on Retell AI's API for creating and managing knowledge bases.

elevenlabs_agents_implementation.description: Details on how ElevenLabs Agents use knowledge bases and RAG for grounded responses.

document_management.description: Comparison of the process for uploading, managing, and updating documents in the knowledge base.

retell_ai_tools.description: Overview of Retell AI's testing suite, including the interactive playground and automated simulation testing.

elevenlabs_agents_tools.description: Overview of the testing and simulation capabilities available for ElevenLabs Agents.

scale_testing.description: Comparison of the ability to perform automated, large-scale testing to validate agent behavior.

retell_ai_analytics.description: Details on Retell's Post Call Analysis feature and the insights it provides.

elevenlabs_agents_analytics.description: Details on the analytics and conversation history features within the ElevenLabs platform.

data_accessibility.description: Comparison of API access to post-call data, including transcripts, logs, and metadata.

retell_ai_events.description: The types of real-time events Retell AI provides via webhooks (e.g., call-related events).

elevenlabs_agents_events.description: The types of conversation events and updates that can be subscribed to in ElevenLabs.

granularity_and_reliability.description: Comparison of the level of detail and reliability of the eventing systems.

retell_ai_compliance.description: Retell AI's compliance certifications, such as SOC 2 and HIPAA, and its BAA availability.

elevenlabs_agents_compliance.description: Information on ElevenLabs Agents' security posture and any available compliance certifications.

data_security_measures.description: Comparison of data security features like transport encryption (TLS), media encryption (SRTP), and data retention policies.

retell_ai_pricing.description: Description of Retell AI's pricing structure, including any known tiers or per-minute costs.

elevenlabs_agents_pricing.description: Description of ElevenLabs' pricing, likely based on character usage for TTS and other API calls.

model_comparison.description: A direct comparison of the two pricing models and how they would apply to a typical voice agent use case.

technical_challenges.description: Key technical hurdles in migrating between platforms, such as differences in WebSocket protocols and tool-calling logic.

feature_parity_gaps.description: An analysis of features unique to one platform that may not have a direct equivalent on the other.

effort_and_cost_estimation.description: A high-level assessment of the development effort and potential cost implications of a migration.
